obligation_text: A risk management system shall be established, implemented, documented and maintained in relation to high-risk AI systems.
scoring_text: Knowledge of AI risk management system design, including establishment, implementation, documentation, and maintenance of risk management processes specific to AI systems throughout the system lifecycle.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI risk management system design, including establishment, implementation, documentation, and maintenance of risk management processes specific to AI systems throughout the system lifecycle.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI governance structures, including model risk management, AI lifecycle oversight, accountability assignment, and the organizational conditions that enable responsible AI deployment at scale.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 9(1) (obligation) equal WIDAI AG domain. AG-K-002 is the strongest match (STS: 0.679, cosine: 0.734). Secondary: AG-K-003 (AG, cosine: 0.725), RC-T-012 (RC, cosine: 0.705).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.8/10 (raw 0.679). Shared vocabulary: [ai, knowledge, lifecycle, management, risk]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [design, documentation, establishment, implementation, maintenance, processes] vs WIDAI terms [accountability, assignment, conditions, deployment, enable, governance] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.903 (P=0.904, R=0.903). Balanced P/R — the 25-word competency implication and 25-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: mixed signal (E:1.2%, N:71.3%, C:27.5%). No dominant NLI classification — the logical relationship between the regulatory requirement and WIDAI KSA is ambiguous.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.734 > STS 0.679 (gap +0.055). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, knowledge, lifecycle, management, risk] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 26.5%. Shared tokens: [ai, knowledge, lifecycle, management, risk]. EU AI Act-only: [design, documentation, establishment, implementation, maintenance, processes]. WIDAI-only: [accountability, assignment, conditions, deployment, enable, governance].
